# A Twitter Research Report

> **The Twitter Loop writes this file (Phase 4) every time you run it.** It's the
> readable digest of all research from that run — growth/trends + how we're
> performing. Open this first after a run.
>
> Workflow: run the **`Refresh Twitter Analysis`** GitHub Action → say **"run the
> twitter loop"** here → read this file + `AA_TWITTER_NEXT_STEPS.md`.

*Seed — not yet generated by a loop run. The structure below is what each run fills in.*

---

## Growth & trends (Phase 1)
- **Viral hook shapes working now:** (whale tape, sharp-vs-public split, honest recap/bounce-back, CLV flex…)
- **Construction tricks:** (first-line tension, ≤3 proof bullets, save-worthy boards, binary close…)
- **Growth levers:** (self-reply in first 30 min, rotate identity beats, one RT line per post…)
- **Trends watchlist:** (matchups/narratives/peer formats spiking this run)

## How we're performing (Phase 2)
- **Labeled sample:** n = ? (directional only)
- **Best post:** [ID] — what worked structurally
- **Worst post:** [ID] — what failed
- **What's working / what's not:** …
- **Niche gap:** the one structural thing peers do better
- **This run's mandates:** …
- **Stop list:** …
